channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: California’s new AI deepfakes law gets blocked by a federal judge. Here’s why
item.description: A new California law allowing any person to sue for damages over election deepfakes has been put on pause after a federal judge granted a preliminary injunction Wednesday blocking it. U.S. District Judge John A.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft admits Windows 11 24H2 could play havoc with some online games – and it’s blocked the update for affected PCs
item.description: Windows 11 24H2 packs some unexpected bugs for PC gamers to watch out for – although Microsoft’s already taken action.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: AI audio wearables have an awkward problem
item.description: The biggest obstacle I’ve run into while reviewing the Plaud NotePin is figuring out what to do with it. This $169 wearable gadget, which is just a bit wider than a AA battery, attaches magnetically to your clothing and records the audio around you.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: Big Fremont office park occupied by German tech titan lands buyer
item.description: A big office and research park in Fremont occupied by a German tech titan has been bought for $70 million-plus.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: Google, it’s time to kill CAPTCHAS
item.description: Are you a robot? Google really, really wants to know. The answer to this question is demanded of web users 200 million times a day via CAPTCHAs — “Completely Automated Public Turing test to tell Computers and Humans Apart,” a system owned and operated by Google.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to use extension methods in C#
item.description: In the C# programming language, extension methods enable you to extend the functionality of existing types without modifying them or deriving new types from them using inheritance.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The role of IoT in shaping smart cities
item.description: As people increasingly migrate from rural areas to cities for better economic opportunities and improved living standards, modern municipalities are faced with challenges.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Snapchat’s failure to protect kids revealed in New Mexico lawsuit
item.description: Snapchat failed to act on “rampant” reports of child grooming, sextortion and other dangers to minors on its platform, according to a newly unredacted complaint against the company filed by New Mexico’s attorney general. Attorney General Raúl Torrez filed the original complaint on Sept.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: How these Grindr competitors are trying to beat the dating app at its own game
item.description: Grindr might not have been the first location-based dating app—that distinction belongs to MeetMoi, a hookup app acquired by Match Group in 2016. But whether LGBT or not, looking for a long-term relationship or not, most singles are living in the house that Grindr built.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: 'Setback' for AI safety
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tesla posts first quarterly increase in deliveries, but shares slump with investors hoping for more
item.description: Low interest financing, sweet lease deals, price cuts and free charging boosted Tesla’s global deliveries in the third quarter, the first increase this year for the electric vehicle maker.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: DoD agencies confront zero trust challenges, misunderstandings ahead of 2026 deadline
item.description: The Defense Department’s zero trust framework is acting as a catalyst, driving mission owners to industry in search of zero trust solutions The post DoD agencies confront zero trust challenges, misunderstandings ahead of 2026 deadline first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Microsoft unveils new AI-powered Windows 11 features for Copilot+ PC users
item.description: Microsoft on Tuesday unveiled the latest AI features for Copilot+ PCs, with improved Windows search and a tool that can detect and act on the contents of a user’s screen.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Docker tutorial: Get started with Docker
item.description: Containers are a lightweight way to make application workloads portable, like a virtual machine but without the overhead and bulk typically associated with VMs. With containers, you can package apps and services and move them freely between physical, virtual, or cloud environments.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Australia's online dating industry adopts code of conduct to keep users safer
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — A code of conduct will be enforced on the online dating industry to better protect Australian users after research found that three-in-four people suffer some form of sexual violence through the platforms, Australia’s government said on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Details from New Mexico's lawsuit against Snap show site failed to act on reports of sextortion
item.description: Snapchat failed to act on “rampant” reports of child grooming, sextortion and other dangers to minors on its platform, according to a newly unredacted complaint against the company filed by New Mexico's attorney general. Attorney General Raúl Torrez filed the original complaint on Sept.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: WWW creator Tim Berners-Lee closes down his foundation
item.description: The World Wide Web Foundation, the organization whose mission has been to make the web safer and more accessible, has shut down, according to The Register. The foundation, which close its virtual doors Sept.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft Copilot gets Voice and Vision features
item.description: Microsoft on Tuesday announced several updates to its free Microsoft Copilot and paid-for Copilot Pro services aimed at making the personal AI assistant more powerful and easier to converse with. Among the updates is Copilot Vision.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: New Jersey offshore wind farm clears big federal hurdle amid environmental concerns
item.description: SEA GIRT, N.J. (AP) — The federal government gave a key approval Tuesday to an offshore wind farm in New Jersey, even as residents in the town where its power cable would come ashore worry it could go through underground toxic waste that's still being cleaned up. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: WAYNE PARRY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Cruise fined $1.5M over robotaxi crash reporting
item.description: General Motors's autonomous vehicle unit, Cruise, will pay a $1.5 million fine after it failed to disclose details initially about a serious crash involving a pedestrian last year, the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Breaking through AI data bottlenecks
item.description: As AI models become increasingly commoditized, the data required to train and fine-tune them has never been more critical.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Germany’s antitrust authority to look into complaint against Microsoft
item.description: German file hosting cloud service provider Nextcloud appeared to have won a major victory Monday, when the country’s antitrust authority announced it would be initiating what the company described as “the first steps towards an investigation into anti-competitive behavior by Microsoft, including the
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: What to stream: Coldplay, an unhinged Charlie Puth, 'Salem’s Lot,' Sarah Paulson and NHL 25
item.description: Coldplay's 10th studio album and the American Music Awards celebrating their 50th anniversary are some of the new television, films, music and games headed to a device near you.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Cyber pay in government is as fragmented as ever
item.description: It’s a case of “haves and have-nots.” Here’s an informal run down of agencies with special cyber pay authorities, including DoD, DHS, and the VA. The post Cyber pay in government is as fragmented as ever first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: The CHIPS Act money: A timeline of grants to chipmakers
item.description: The CHIPS and Science Act provides $52.7 billion for American semiconductor research, development, manufacturing, and workforce development.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Arm, Intel, and the vengeance of Newton
item.description: Arm’s attempt to purchase part of Intel’s computer chip business may have been declined, but it once again demonstrates the important role played in the evolution of today’s tech by Apple and its handheld Newton.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Verizon, AT&T, T-Mobile service outages reported by users
item.description: People in multiple cities posted about issues they were having, with many saying their phones were in "SOS" mode.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Explained: How Salesforce Agentforce’s Atlas reasoning engine works to power AI agents
item.description: Salesforce’s recently launched Agentforce suite of low-code tools is betting on a human-like reasoning engine called Atlas that could be a game-changer in building AI agents.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft gives enterprises new reasons to adopt Fabric
item.description: Microsoft is working to make its cloud-based Fabric combined offering of data warehousing, data science, data engineering, and data analytics services more attractive, previewing a host of new features at its first annual European Microsoft Fabric Community Conference last week.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: California governor vetoes bill to create first-in-nation AI safety measures
item.description: The decision is a blow to efforts attempting to rein in the industry that is rapidly evolving with little oversight.
item.dc:creator: TRÂN NGUYỄN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Brazil judge makes new requests to allow X to be reinstated from suspension
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazilian Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes on Friday added conditions for Elon Musk’s X to have its service reestablished in the country, one day after the social media platform said it had complied with all the judge’s demands, including naming a legal representative.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Security, AIOps top mainframe customer challenges, BMC says
item.description: Compliance, security, operational analytics, and AIOps are the top priorities for organizations investing heavily in mainframes, according to the 2024 BMC Mainframe Survey, which is in its 19th year and polled 1,000 customers.
item.dc:creator: Network World
